rows.description: Computus Gu: Paynter Rectory a secundo die
rows.notes: Top-most line, clear page title. Handwriting legible and no numbers on this line.
Continuation of the page heading (date). Clear and centered; no monetary amounts.

rows.description: Imprimis redditus comput: de remanentib: in Lista
rows.notes: Numbers at far right read as '11 9 4' with a small loop/ob after the 4 interpreted as the halfpenny sign (0.5). Vertical ruling lines are visible; alignment with the total row confirms columns. Some minor ambiguity in the tiny loop for halfpenny but placement & common notation support 4½d.
Clear reading: '60 0 0' aligned with the pounds/shilling/pence columns. Ink and numerals unambiguous.
Numbers clearly read as '76 13 4' and align well to the columns. All currency ranges valid.
'20 0 0' is clear in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; handwriting for the place name is legible and the amount alignment is unambiguous.
Amount '30 0 0' visible and aligned with the row. The place-name spelling is approximate to the handwriting but the amount is clear.

A small amount is visible adjacent to this row. The numerals read as '03 0 8' and there is a small loop after the 8 reminiscent of the halfpenny sign; however the alignment is slightly uncertain because of a marginal scribble ('pro mi' or similar) above the dashes. Assigned 3s.0s.8½d with moderate confidence.
Numerals '02 15 7' are visible and align with this row. Ink is clear; currency fields within valid ranges.
Right-hand numerals read as '03 8 11' aligned with this line. The '11d' is clear; slight ink thinning in the shillings but placement confirms reading.
Line shows '04 0 0' fairly clearly opposite 'Thrup'. The handwriting of the name is a bit stylized but the amount alignment is consistent.
Amount read as '01 12 0' aligned with this text. The phrase looks like 'Tue quitrents' (older spelling); numbers are clear and valid.
Shillings read as '10' with pounds 0, pence 0 (displayed as '00 10 0'). Name is somewhat abbreviated/condensed; amount alignment is moderately clear.
Numerals read as '00 15 0' opposite this entry. The place name is short and clear; amount alignment unambiguous.
Amount '03 8 11' is visible and well-aligned with the 'Bampton' row; digits are legible and in valid ranges.
'04 0 0' is visible opposite this entry; handwriting of place is slightly stylized but amount alignment is clear.

There is an amount close to this row that reads '01 12 0'. However the ink is a little lighter here and the exact association to 'Hughes' vs. the adjacent line is slightly uncertain; assigned based on vertical alignment of digits.
An amount '00 10 0' appears aligned near this row; ink is light and spacing tight, so association is moderately uncertain.
There is clear '00 15 0' near these lines; because several short names are clustered, column alignment makes the assignment plausible but not unequivocal.

The name 'Minne' (or similar) is legible but I cannot confidently read a distinct amount for this exact line — the area has fewer visible digits and several nearby entries share close alignment. Left blank rather than risk mis-assignment.
Name is legible (Leaden-hall). No clear numerals directly opposite this line; possible the amount is included as part of a grouped subtotal elsewhere. Left amounts empty to avoid misreading.
Name (Molineux) visible; amount is not unambiguously visible on the scanned image at the precise horizontal alignment for this row. Omitted to avoid incorrect allocation.

Tintinhull appears with a brace listing 'Napper' on the same vertical group. The first of the two numbers nearby reads roughly as '02 5 5' (pounds, shillings, pence). Digits are a bit cramped; chosen reading is plausible though the pence digit is slightly unclear.
Second entry under the Tintinhull brace reads clearly as '01 18 7' (1 pound, 18 shillings, 7 pence). Numerals are fairly distinct and align with the 'Hopkins' line.
Right-hand digits read well as '03 19 8' adjacent to 'Clifton Ferry'. Alignment is clear and values are in range.
Amount reads '01 15 6' across from 'Bensington' (or similar spelling). Numerals are clear and columns unambiguous.
The amount '06 9 7' is visible near the bottom portion of the list and aligns with 'Brewhouse' (handwriting reads 'Brew house'). Slight ink fade but digits are legible.

The place-name at the next line (appearing as 'Trehlewin' or similar) is legible, but no distinct amount is readable in its specific horizontal line — likely included in a grouping; left blank to avoid mis-assignment.

Final textual entry 'Merton vicar.' is readable. There is no clearly separate numeric line directly opposite; sum may be incorporated elsewhere on the page. Amount left blank due to unclear alignment.

Bottom right total clearly reads '230 13 8'. The word preceding it (Summa...) is partially abbreviated but context and alignment make this the page subtotal/total. Numerals are crisp and well-aligned with the ruling lines.
